Propósito: Electrospin cellulose acetate using class 3 solvets only
Tipo de Drum: The one that's normally on the LE-100
Comentarios del Proceso: Sol'n gels and dries around the needle, even at 60% humidity and 2.5 mL/h.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra: Decent fibers (but process was terrible)

Propósito: Same as above
Comentarios del Proceso:
Displays a jet 0.5-1 cm long before it spirals out, as do all samples below.
Process is stable.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Fibers + Casting (maybe beads as well).
Sol'n is too heavy and WD is too small.

Propósito: Same as above
Comentarios del Proceso: Process is stable.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Particles + Casting, the occasional fiber.
Polymer concentration is too low.

Propósito: Same as above
Comentarios del Proceso:
Higher V => Spiral "dances".
Higher Q => Dripping.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra: Extremely beaded fibers. Concentration is still too low, but it’s not as bad. The lighter solvent mixture is better, as no casting is observed, meaning fibers had time to dry before reaching the collector.

Propósito: Increase polymer concentration to avoid beaded fibers
Comentarios del Proceso: Sol'n is not stable.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra: Somewhat beaded fibers, much better than the 13% sol'n.

Propósito: Same as above + incrase flow rate as well to see if it helps avoiding beaded fibers
Comentarios del Proceso: Sol'n is not stable.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra: Somewhat beaded fibers, much better than the 13% sol'n.

Propósito: Eliminate beading observed above
Comentarios del Proceso: Before T and H settled, occasional dripping was observed, which was reduced upon slightly increasing the voltage.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra: More beaded than the 15% sol'n

Propósito: Same, at a higher flow rate
Comentarios del Proceso: Same as above.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Even more beaded than at 0.6 mL/h.
Beads are fusing into fibers, indicating they landed wet onto the mat

Propósito: Eliminate beading
Comentarios del Proceso: Little no no dripping (but dripping at higher flow rates), spiral dances a little bit
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra: Beaded fibers, possibly due to the low flow rate.

Propósito: Eliminate beading and dripping
Comentarios del Proceso: No dripping, but spiral dances a bit more than for the larger needle (hence voltage was decreased)
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra: Beaded fibers, possibly due to the low flow rate.

Propósito: Eliminate beading
Comentarios del Proceso: Spiral dances a tiny bit, stabilizes with time.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra: Beaded fibers. Fibers are dry, beads are somewhat fused (wet).

Propósito: Eliminate beading
Comentarios del Proceso: Spiral dances a tiny bit, stabilizes with time.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra: Beaded fibers. Fibers are dry, beads are slightly fused (less than #12)

Propósito: Eliminate beading by increasing V
Comentarios del Proceso: Spiral dances a lot.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra: Beaded fibers. Fibers are dry, beads are somewhat fused (wet).

Propósito: Eliminate beading by increasing polymer conc.
Comentarios del Proceso:
Spiral is stable.
Ecu is unstable: 19%<H<24%
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Beautiful fibers, zero beading, zero fusing.
Fiber diameter: µ=330, stdev=90 nm

Propósito: Eliminate beading by increasing both polymer conc. AND voltage.
Comentarios del Proceso:
Spiral is at an angle but stable (little to no dancing), in spite of the high voltage - higher stability than #14.
Ecu is unstable: 19%<H<24%
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Nearly as good as #15, with a few artifacts.
Fiber diameter: 200-620 nm

Propósito: Make thinner fibers by lowering Q
Comentarios del Proceso:
Spiral is stable.
Ecu is semi-stable: 20%<H<22%
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Good fibers, but there are more defects than for #15 (0.6 mL/h).
Fiber diameter: 140-1070 nm

Propósito: Upscale to one injector of 20 needles
Comentarios del Proceso:
Almost perfectly stable.
Dripping at lower voltages.
Tiny amount of spiral dancing.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Fibers. More defects than for a single needle. Zero beading.
Fiber diameter: µ=500, stdev=150 nm

Propósito: Upscale beyond one injector
Comentarios del Proceso:
Note: injectors at pos. 4, 7, 10 (left=>right).
Edge effects near right injector, jets are less stable there. Otherwise jets are fairly stable.
"Puntitos" on material - indicating micro-dripping or a similar problem.
Mat is heterogeneous (probably due to net-type collector - changed after #22).
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Fibers. More defects than for a single needle. Zero beading.
Fiber diameter: µ=460, stdev=150 nm

Propósito: Upscale beyond one injector
Comentarios del Proceso:
Note: injectors at pos. 4, 7, 10 (left=>right).
Same as #19.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Fibers. More defects than for a single needle. Zero beading.
Fiber diameter: µ=460, stdev=150 nm

Propósito: Upscale beyond one injector
Comentarios del Proceso:
Note: injectors at pos. 3, 6, 9 (left=>right).
Note: Also tried pos. 4, 6, 8: dripping at central injector, so spaces of 3 are required.
Edge effects eliminated!
Otherwise same as #19.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Fibers. More defects than for a single needle. Zero beading.
Fiber diameter: µ=530, stdev=150 nm

Propósito: Static: compare three injectors
Comentarios del Proceso:
Static test to see differences between the 3 injectors.
#22 L, C, R: left, central, right injectors, respectively.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra: Some areas are pure casting (static)

Propósito: Belt collector instead of metal netting - for a more homogeneous mat
Comentarios del Proceso:
Jets look mostly fine, with the occational instability.
Mat is still textured and heterogeneous - the belt collector helps but not enough.
Some points on mat imply dripping.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Better than the previous 20 and 60 needle samples - pure fibers, very few defects.
Fiber diameter: µ=490, stdev=130 nm

Propósito: Eliminate material banding by replacing belt collector with a solid metal plate collector (belt has some damage).
Comentarios del Proceso:
Jets look good.
"Puntitos" on mat - Voltage might be too high.
Again, small-scale texture.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Good fibers, few imperfections. Possibly fewer imperfections on top than on bottom.
Fiber diameter: µ=550, stdev=170 nm

Propósito: Eliminte "puntios" found in #24 by lowering V (optimal V expected to decrease with fewer needles anyway)
Comentarios del Proceso:
Jets look good. One neddle was dripping at 19-(-16)kV. Dripping at 20-(-16) as well!
Near-right deflector rotated upwards.
All mats on PP show a texture (see on the right)
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Good fibers, few imperfections. Possibly fewer imperfections on top than on bottom.
Fiber diameter: µ=540, stdev=160 nm

Propósito: Test effect of Q (and, by necessity, V) on mat
Comentarios del Proceso:
Jets look good, but process seems on the edge of instability.
Deflectors back to normal.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Decent fibers, some thicker fibers fusing into their neighbours. Flow rate issue?
Fiber diameter: µ=570, stdev=220 nm

Propósito: See texture with siliconized paper on perforated metal collector.
Comentarios del Proceso:
Done with one deflector rotated up to prevent dripping at its nearest needle.
Some instability, some dripping.
The texture that was visible on PP is almost or completely gone.
Some striping along the x-axis, probably due to perforations on metal collector.
Some puntitos.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Good fibers, few imperfections.
Fiber diameter: µ=490, stdev=160 nm

Propósito: See if process is more stable and mat is more homogeneous on conudctive PE
Comentarios del Proceso:
Process looks stable.
Texture is either almost or completely gone.
No striping.
Puntitos are still there.
Better than siliconized paper.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Good fibers, few to no imperfections.
Fiber diameter: µ=480, stdev=150 nm

Propósito: See if texture still appears on 25 gsm PET
Comentarios del Proceso:
Jets look good.
Material: many many "puntitos"
Stripes correlating exactly to the perforations in the metal (2.8-2.9 stripes/cm)

Propósito: See if puntitos are eliminated by turning sweep off
Comentarios del Proceso:
Jets look good.
Material: fewer "puntitas", but of course no sweep => lines on material, not good. Sweep appaers to be mandatory.

Propósito: Get rid of stripes caused by perforated metal collector.
Comentarios del Proceso:
Jets look good.
Paper develops folds.
Some puntitos.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Top shows fewer defects than bottom.
Fiber diameter: µ=500, stdev=180 nm

Propósito: [Not a proper sample] optimize V and paper positioning
Comentarios del Proceso: Possibly some casting

Propósito: Prevent casting around center by decreasing Q, increasing T, decreasing H
Comentarios del Proceso:
Mostly fine. "Puntitos" coming from a few specific needles near the centers of both injectors.
One "magic wanding" of a needle => a bunch of droplets and puntitos.

Propósito:
Eliminate "puntitos", folds, dripping, instabilities, material loss.
Removed needles # 1, 19, 37 from injectors.
Comentarios del Proceso:
Jets look good. Some of the time, the back jets go outside of the substrate [after ~45 min, it happened more]. No substrate folding unless pressing "jog".
Still some "puntitos" on mat!
One needle near center of left injector showing a large taylor cone / potential drop, but not as bad as before removing neddles #19 from injector.
Some "mountain" growing outside of belt.
Lower RTR velocity => less pronounced "mountains", just for the amount of material.
Stripes on mat (visible through light) - tension probably too high.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Decent fibers.
Bottom was difficult to focus on SEM as it wasn't flat (imaging issue, not material issue).
Bottom also shows thinner fibers, and is under-represented in the overall data for being harder to measure:
Bottom: µ=350, stdev=70 nm (n=45)
Top: µ=500, stdev=160 nm (n=82)
Total: µ=450, stdev=160 nm

Propósito:
Obtain thinner fibers and more stable process by having needles further apart.
Eliminate lines by increasing tension on SiO2 paper.
Comentarios del Proceso:
Process: no dripping, a couple of needles with large taylor cones, but generally fine and better than before removing half of the needles. Some of the time, material is attracted to outside of the collector. Sometimes fiber bridges are formed.
Mat: Showing stripes along the x-direction, probably due to tension on the SiO2 paper (appeared in both 10% and 40% tension).
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Decent fibers.
Imaging bottom of sample gives skewed results.
Top: µ=560 nm, stdev=140 nm
Total: µ=530, stdev=120 nm

Propósito:
See if injectors can be places closer together (no spacing).
Also work at a reduced Q.
Comentarios del Proceso: 7-8 of the 34 needles at the two central injectors show borderline dripping, in spite of the lower Q. V increased to compensate with partial success but creating problems of threading (fiber bridging from needles to material).

Propósito:
Reduce Q to stabilize process.
Increase sweep to avoid lines (even at the cost of material being lost to outside the collector).
With no deflectors
Comentarios del Proceso: Too much chaos with no deflectors.

Propósito:
Reduce Q to stabilize process.
Increase sweep to avoid lines (even at the cost of material being lost to outside the collector).
With deflectors
Shift sweep to prevent material going out of collector (eventually it can create bridges)
Comentarios del Proceso:
Better than previous. Deflectors help. Some material is still lost, probably due to the larger sweep range, but it's worth it if itgets rid of the lines on the mat.
Sweep shifted by 10 mm towards operator to prevent material loss - because material loss also formed a bridge from deflector to side of collector. Following that change, a bridge from deflector to collector was formed several times on the near-operator side, and had to be manually removed each time.
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Fibers look good.
Imaging bottom of sample gives skewed results.
Top: µ=500 nm, stdev=130 nm

Propósito: 10 A4 samples to send to Sefar
Comentarios del Proceso:
10 A4-size sheets sent to sefar.
Jets look good.
Mat: Decent but not perfect, some puntitos.
One bad needle (left-most injector, fifth needle form the far side) was intermitant between spinning and sputtering, horribly maring the mat. Needle replaced, injectors re-leveled, sample restarted.
Reduced max sweep to 270 as material kept making bridges between collector and deflectors. It helped for a few minutes.
Material was bridging on both sides of the collector (sometimes one, sometimes the other, sometimes both, sometimes neither).
Comentarios SEM / Tamaño de Fibra:
Fluffy samples harder to SEM well.
Fibers look good.
Values might be skewed towards thinner fibers due to sample being fluffy:
µ=470 nm, stdev=100 nm
